description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the lighter, rough-textured stone material in between. The block has a solid, robust appearance, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the focus on the grooved top. The coloration is primarily a mix of beige and dark brown or black, giving it a weathered, utilitarian look. Such a design suggests it is intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle, possibly for urban or architectural use.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The block is solid, with a rough stone texture and contrasting dark grooves.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide face of the block with horizontal grooves and a striped pattern.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow face and the depth of the vertical grooves.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the horizontal grooves and striped surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the narrow face and the vertical grooves' depth.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
